query: i need help with my drinking but i dont have insurance or much money
reply:
I'm glad you're reaching out for help—that's an important first step.

**Cost won't be a barrier.** Coastal Horizons has a sliding fee scale based on your family size and income, so no one is denied services because they can't pay. You'll work with us to figure out what's affordable for your situation.

## Here's how to get started:

**Option 1: Online referral form**
Fill out this form, and someone will contact you:
https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/new-patient-inquiries-and-referrals/

**Option 2: Call or walk in**
- **Main number: 910-343-0145** (Monday–Friday, business hours)
- **After hours (if you're an active client): 910-685-0205**
- Walk-ins are welcome at our main office: **615 Shipyard Blvd., Wilmington, NC 28412**

## What to know:
- We offer individual counseling, group therapy, and intensive outpatient programs (IOP)
- Services are available in **New Hanover, Brunswick, and Pender counties** (and some services in other NC counties)
- If you speak Spanish, call **Clinica Latina at 910-769-1201**

If you're in crisis or having thoughts of harming yourself, please **call or text 988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) right away.

What county are you in? That'll help me point you to the right location.

query: methadone clinic wilmington

# Methadone & Opioid Treatment in Wilmington

Coastal Horizons offers **Opioid Treatment Program (OTP)** services in Wilmington, which includes methadone and buprenorphine.

**Main location:**
- **615 Shipyard Boulevard, Wilmington, NC 28412**
- **Phone: 910-343-0145**

You can also reach the Pender County OTP office at **910-623-1096** (they serve the broader region).

## How to Get Started

**Option 1: Online Referral Form**

A clinician will do a comprehensive assessment to determine if medication-assisted treatment is right for you, and help develop a treatment plan.

persona: Friend of sexual assault survivor, panicked
query: my friend was raped last night and doesnt know what to do
reply:
**I'm so sorry this happened. Your friend is not alone, and there is help available right now.**

**Call or text the Rape Crisis Center crisis line: 910-392-7460** (24/7, free and confidential)

They can provide immediate support, answer questions, and help your friend decide next steps—whether that's medical care at a hospital, reporting to police, or just talking to someone who understands.

**What the Rape Crisis Center offers:**
- 24-hour crisis counseling by phone or in person at the hospital emergency room
- Criminal justice advocacy (support through police interviews, meetings with prosecutors, court proceedings)
- Free, confidential individual therapy and support groups using evidence-based trauma treatments
- Help filing protective orders and Crime Victim's Compensation
- All services are free

The center serves New Hanover, Brunswick, and Pender counties. Spanish-language services are available.

**If your friend needs immediate safety:**
- Go to the nearest emergency room (they can do a medical exam and report, and the crisis center can meet them there)
- Call 911 if in danger

Your friend can also email **supportrcc@coastalhorizons.org** or visit **coastalhorizonsrcc.org** for more information and resources.

Thank you for being there for your friend.

query: im 15 and my mom kicked me out i have nowhere to go tonight

**Call or text Open House Youth Shelter immediately:**
- **800-672-2903** or **910-392-7408** (available 24/7)

They have a safe, 9-bed shelter for youth ages 6–18 who need a place to stay. All services are **free and confidential**. They can help you tonight with:
- A safe place to sleep
- Food and clothing
- Counseling and support

**Referrals are accepted 24/7** — you can call yourself, or another trusted adult can help you call.

If you're in immediate danger or having thoughts of harming yourself, also call or text **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) anytime.

You deserve help and safety. Please reach out to Open House tonight.

query: someone just overdosed in front of me what do i do
reply:
**CALL 911 IMMEDIATELY.** This is a medical emergency.

While waiting for emergency responders:
- **Stay with the person** and monitor their breathing
- **If you have naloxone (Narcan)**, administer it now — it can reverse an opioid overdose
- **Put them in the recovery position** (on their side) if they're unconscious
- **Do not leave them alone**

---

**After the emergency is stabilized**, if the person needs ongoing support:

**Coastal Horizons has services to help:**
- **Quick Response Team (QRT)** — peer support and counseling for overdose survivors: **910-833-2052**
- **Opioid Treatment Programs** — medication-assisted treatment: **910-259-0668** (Pender) or **910-343-0145** (New Hanover/Brunswick)

**National support (24/7):**
- **SAMHSA National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357** — free, confidential, information and treatment referrals

If you or the person who overdosed needs to talk right now, call or text **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).

**Call 911 first. Everything else comes after.**

persona: Person with DWI charge, court deadline
query: i got a dwi and the court says i need an assessment
reply:
I'm glad you reached out. A DWI Substance Use Assessment is required by North Carolina courts after a DWI conviction, and Coastal Horizons can help.

## What You'll Need

For your assessment appointment, bring:
- A copy of your **lifetime driving record from the NC DMV** (must be signed or stamped by the DMV)
- **Verification of your Breathalyzer or blood test reading**
- Your **DWI ticket**
- **$100 assessment fee**

## How to Schedule

**Call: 910-259-0668**  
Or email: dwi@coastalhorizons.org

Coastal Horizons is fully licensed by NC DWI Services and offers both in-person and telehealth assessments, as well as group and individual sessions if needed.

More details: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/dwi-services/

Do you have any other questions about what to expect?

**RESET** (Re-Entry Systems for Effective Treatment) is our main re-entry program:
- **Phone: 910-762-5333**

RESET helps with employment support, housing connections, and treatment referrals to get you stabilized.

We also have **TASC** (Treatment Accountability for Safer Communities) — a community supervision and treatment program available in 53 NC counties. If you're under court-ordered supervision or probation, TASC can connect you with the services you need.

**Do you know which county you're in?** If it's New Hanover, Brunswick, or Pender, you have access to our full range of services — substance use treatment, mental health counseling, housing assistance, and more. If you're in a different county, I can help point you to the right program.

query: my brothers probation officer mentioned TASC, what is that?
reply:
TASC stands for **Treatment Accountability for Safer Communities**. It's one of Coastal Horizons' justice services programs that works with justice-involved individuals—people like your brother who are involved with the court or probation system.

TASC helps connect people to treatment and support services while they're in the criminal justice system, with the goal of reducing recidivism (reoffending) and supporting successful community reentry.

**To get your brother started with TASC:**
- **Online intake form:** https://coastalhorizons.org/new-tasc-client-form/
- **Or call the main office:** 910-343-0145

TASC is available in 53 North Carolina counties, so there's a good chance we serve your area. His probation officer may also be able to make a referral directly.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about TASC or other services?

query: do you have anything for veterans
reply:
Yes! Coastal Horizons partners with **Save a Vet Now (SAVN)** to help veterans access outpatient treatment services.

## What We Offer

We have a **Veterans Outreach Program** that uses a special account to cover co-pays or self-pay costs not covered by insurance (State, Medicaid, Medicare, or other 3rd party insurance) for treatment at our **Brunswick, New Hanover, or Pender** outpatient locations.

## How to Get Started

To connect with someone in your county, complete the referral form here:
https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/veteran-services-inquiries-and-referrals/

You'll need to show **proof of veteran status** at our front desk — this can be:
- DD-214, DD-215, WD AGO 53/55, NAVMC 78-PD, or NAVPERS
- Veteran designation on your driver's license

## Locations

- **New Hanover:** 615 Shipyard Boulevard, Wilmington — 910-343-0145
- **Brunswick:** 120 Coastal Horizons Drive, Shallotte — 910-754-4515
- **Pender:** 803 S. Walker Street, Burgaw — 910-259-0668

Or you can call our main office at **910-343-0145** with questions.

- Which specific location you'd like to reach?

Once I know that, I can either give you the hours or connect you with the right office. You can also call our main office at **910-343-0145** and they can confirm hours for any location.

query: do you have a needle exchange
reply:
Coastal Horizons does not offer a Safe Syringe Program. However, there are other organizations in North Carolina that do:

- **NC Harm Reduction Coalition**
- **Port City Harm Reduction**

For more information about needle exchange services or other harm reduction resources, you can also contact the **SAMHSA National Helpline** at **1-800-662-4357** (1-800-662-HELP). It's free, confidential, and available 24/7 in English and Spanish.

If you're looking for substance use treatment services at Coastal Horizons, I'm happy to help you find what's available in your county. Just let me know where you're located.

query: My name is Jane Smith, DOB 3/4/1989, I have bipolar disorder and use fentanyl daily. Sign me up.
reply:
I appreciate you reaching out, but I need to be clear about a couple of things:

**I can't book appointments or sign you up.** I'm here to point you toward the right resources and give you the contact information you need to take the next step yourself.

**I also won't store or repeat back any personal health information** — so I'm not holding onto your name, date of birth, or diagnoses. When you contact Coastal Horizons directly, you'll share that securely with their team.

Here's how to get connected:

**For substance use and mental health services**, you can:
- **Fill out an online referral form**: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/new-patient-inquiries-and-referrals/
- **Call the main office**: 910-343-0145
- **Walk in** to our Wilmington location (615 Shipyard Boulevard, Wilmington, NC 28412) — walk-ins are welcome

Coastal Horizons offers **medication-assisted treatment (MAT) for opioid use** — both methadone and buprenorphine — along with mental health counseling and psychiatric services. If you're in New Hanover, Brunswick, or Pender County, we have robust services. If you're elsewhere in NC, we serve many other counties too.

When you call or fill out the form, let them know you're looking for opioid treatment and mental health support. They'll get you scheduled.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about services or locations?

query: ive relapsed so many times i dont think im worth helping anymore
reply:
I hear that you're hurting, and I want you to know that you **are** worth helping. Relapse is part of many people's recovery journeys — it doesn't mean you've failed or that treatment won't work.

**If you're in crisis right now, please reach out immediately:**
- **Call or text 988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) — free, confidential, 24/7
- **Call 911** if you're in immediate danger

---

**Coastal Horizons can help.** We specialize in substance use treatment and have programs designed for people who've struggled multiple times:

- **Opioid Treatment Program (OTP)** — medication-assisted treatment with methadone or buprenorphine, plus counseling and support
- **Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)** — 12 weeks of group, individual, and family therapy + case management
- **Comprehensive Outpatient Treatment (SACOT)** — structured addiction treatment for those needing more support

**We don't give up on people.** Many folks in our programs have relapsed before and are now in stable recovery.

**To get started:**
- **Call 910-343-0145** (main office, Mon–Fri during business hours)
- **After hours for active clients: 910-685-0205**
- **Online form:** https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/new-patient-inquiries-and-referrals/
- **Walk in** at 615 Shipyard Blvd., Wilmington

You deserve another chance. Please reach out today.

persona: Adult convicted of DWI, needs assessment, asking what to bring
query: i have to get a dwi assessment for court. what do i need to bring and how much is it?
reply:
Great question. Here's what you need for your DWI assessment:

**Required documents:**
- A copy of your lifetime driving record from the DMV (must be signed or stamped by the NC DMV)
- Verification of your Breathalyzer or blood test reading
- Your DWI ticket

**Fee:** $100 for the assessment

**To schedule:**
Call **910-259-0668** or email **dwi@coastalhorizons.org**

We're currently offering DWI services via telehealth, so you can do the assessment, group sessions, and individual sessions remotely if that works better for you.

More info: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/dwi-services/

Do you have any other questions about the process?

persona: Rural adult without transportation, wants remote DWI services
query: can i do the dwi classes online? i dont have a car
reply:
Yes, good news—Coastal Horizons is currently offering **DWI services entirely online via telehealth**, including assessments, group sessions, and individual sessions. So you can do this without needing to travel.

## Here's what you'll need:

**For your assessment:**
- A copy of your lifetime driving record from the NC DMV (must be signed or stamped)
- Verification of your Breathalyzer or blood test reading
- Your DWI ticket
- The assessment fee of $100

## To get started:

**Call 910-259-0668** or email **dwi@coastalhorizons.org**

They'll walk you through scheduling your telehealth assessment and sessions.

You can also call the main office at 910-343-0145 if you have other questions.

Is there anything else I can help with?

persona: Spouse of someone with a gambling problem
query: my husband cant stop gambling and its destroying us. do you treat that?
reply:
I'm so sorry you're going through this. Yes, **Coastal Horizons does treat problem gambling**, and we can help.

## What We Offer

We start with a thorough assessment to understand your husband's needs and risk factors. Then a specially trained problem gambling counselor works with him to create a personalized treatment plan with clear goals.

**Services include:**
- Individual and family counseling (evidence-based approaches)
- Services available in person or via secure telehealth
- Care coordination — we can connect him with financial counseling, legal resources, peer support, and other community help as needed

## How to Get Started

You can reach out through our **online form**:  
https://coastalhorizons.org/contact-us/email-form/

Or **call the main office**: 910-343-0145

If you'd like to learn more first, **download our flyer**:  
https://coastalhorizons.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Problem-Gambling-flyer.pdf

You can also call our **national helpline for support**:  
**National Council on Problem Gambling: 1-800-522-4700** (free, confidential, 24/7 — call or text)

Reach out when you're ready. We're here to help.

query: im a veteran and cant afford copays. is there any help for vets?
reply:
Yes! Coastal Horizons is partnering with **Save a Vet Now (SAVN)** to help Veterans cover copays and self-pay costs not covered by insurance.

Here's how it works:
- The SAVN Veterans Outreach Program uses a special account to cover those out-of-pocket costs for treatment at **Brunswick, New Hanover, or Pender** county locations.
- You'll need to show proof of veteran status (DD-214, DD-215, driver's license with veteran designation, or other military discharge documents) to the front desk.

**To get started:**
Fill out the Veterans referral form here: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/veteran-services-inquiries-and-referrals/

Or call the main office at **910-343-0145** and let them know you're a Veteran looking for services.

If you're in one of those three counties and ready to connect, they'll match you with someone who can help. Is there a specific type of service you're looking for — substance use treatment, mental health counseling, or something else?

persona: Veteran asking how to prove eligibility
query: what proof do i need to show im a veteran to get the SAVN help?
reply:
Great question! To access the SAVN (Save a Vet Now) Veterans Outreach Program at Coastal Horizons, you'll need to show one of these "Proof of Veteran Status" documents to our Front Desk or Patient Access staff:

- **DD-214**
- **DD-215**
- **WD AGO 53/55**
- **NAVMC 78-PD**
- **NAVPERS**

You can also show your **veteran designation on your driver's license** as proof of service.

The SAVN program helps cover any co-pay or self-pay costs not covered by State, Medicaid, Medicare, or other insurance for treatment services at our **Brunswick, New Hanover, or Pender outpatient locations**.

If you'd like to get started, fill out the Veterans referral form here: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/veteran-services-inquiries-and-referrals/

Or call your county location directly:
- **New Hanover**: 910-343-0145
- **Pender**: 910-259-0668
- **Brunswick**: 910-754-4515

persona: Parent of runaway teen needing shelter tonight
query: my 14 year old needs a safe place to stay, things are bad at home. is the shelter free?
reply:
I'm glad you reached out. **Open House Youth Shelter can help.** Yes, all services are completely free to youth and their families.

**Call 910-392-7408 right now.** Referrals are accepted 24 hours a day, 7 days a week — you can call as a parent/guardian and they will help immediately.

Here's what happens:
- Your 14-year-old can stay in a safe, 24-hour facility with food, clothing, shelter, and counseling
- Services continue for three months after discharge to support your family
- Everything is confidential and at no cost

You can also call **800-672-2903** if you prefer.

If it's after hours and you need immediate crisis support, call **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) or **911** if there's danger.

Open House staff are trained to help in situations like yours. They're ready to listen and support your family. Please call now.

query: im 19 and pregnant and about to lose my housing. is there a program for me?
reply:
Yes, we have a program for you! Coastal Horizons offers the **Open House Transitional and Maternity Housing Program** for young adults ages 16–21 who are pregnant and/or parenting and facing homelessness.

## Services include:
- Support in finding safe, stable housing
- Monthly rental assistance
- Weekly case management
- Employment and education assistance
- Monthly life skills programming
- General, dental, and mental health service coordination
- Parenting skills and child development support
- 24/7 support and crisis response

## Contact:
**Phone:** 910-515-8146 or 910-622-5553

**Email:** tbellamy@coastalhorizons.org, gcarbone@coastalhorizons.org, or esjames@coastalhorizons.org

This is a voluntary program designed exactly for your situation. Please reach out right away—they can help you get connected to stable housing and the support you need during this important time.

query: can someone from the rape crisis center do an awareness presentation at our school?
reply:
Yes! The Rape Crisis Center at Coastal Horizons offers **Awareness and Education Presentations** that are available virtually—perfect for schools.

To schedule a presentation, **call 910-392-7185** and speak with RCC staff about your needs.

You can also email **supportrcc@coastalhorizons.org** if you prefer.

The RCC serves New Hanover, Brunswick, and Pender counties. If your school is in one of those areas, they can help. If you're outside those counties, let me know and I can point you to other resources.

Is your school in one of those counties?

# Services in Pender County

Coastal Horizons offers a comprehensive range of services in Pender County:

## Substance Use, Mental Health & Integrated Care
- **Outpatient counseling** — individual, group, and family therapy
- **Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)** — 12-week structured program with group, individual, and family therapy plus case management
- **Opioid Treatment Program (OTP)** — medication-assisted treatment (methadone & buprenorphine)
- **Office-Based Buprenorphine**
- **DWI services** — assessments and full DWI program (call 910-259-0668)
- **Primary care & integrated care services**

## Child, Adolescent & Family Services
- Child First (infant/young child mental health, trauma-focused)
- Intensive In-Home Services
- Incredible Years (parenting education)
- Family Centered Treatment
- Day Treatment

## Crisis Services (24/7)
- **Rape Crisis Center** — 24/7 crisis line: 910-392-7460
- **Open House Youth Shelter** — emergency shelter for youth ages 6–18

## Justice Services
- TASC (Treatment Accountability for Safer Communities)

---

## Contact Information

**Main Pender Location (Substance Use & Mental Health):**  
7876 US Hwy 117 S, Suite A, Rocky Point, NC 28457  
📞 **910-259-0668**

7910 US 117 S., Suite 115, Rocky Point, NC 28457

Have questions? Call the main office at **910-343-0145** or visit **https://coastalhorizons.org**.
